08/05/26 How far away or how close are we to having AI Agents managing humans? We're already close to AI agents "managing humans" — assigning tasks, setting priorities, recommending decisions managers often follow. In some roles, people have shifted from doing the work to supervising the AI that does it. But running entire organisations without human oversight? Not yet. Humans still decide what matters and make the judgement calls. The real limiter isn't capability — it's governance, accountability and trust.

08/05/26 Can AI systems care? AI can listen, adapt, and respond with what feels like empathy—tutoring a student in the way that clicks, or guiding a patient through symptoms with calm, thoughtful questions. As it grows more agentic, it can even feel like a teammate. 
 
But it doesn't replace the clinician, teacher, or colleague. Humans bring accountability and real empathy. The magic happens when thoughtful people and thoughtful tech work side by side. Brenda Vethanayagam, Partner, AI, Trust Leader, PwC Canada

08/05/26 One AI for all, or many competing platforms? Honestly? We won't land on one "shared" AI—and that's a good thing. Different models suit different people and tasks. Some excel at quick wins; others go deeper on reasoning or creativity. 
 
What matters is the layer around them. At PwC, ChatPwC gives our people secure access to leading AI models in one place—powered by PwC's collective brain, so they tap the right tool, grounded in our standards. Pete Brown, Global Workforce Leader, PwC

08/05/26 What was the key idea of the conference? It's time to move past the AI hype and focus on real impact—how this technology helps us work smarter, solve tougher problems, and create genuine value, without losing sight of the human questions it raises. 
 
"All of Us" means this future can't be built by a few. As AI converges with robotics and quantum, it'll take diverse voices and disciplines to shape something truly inclusive, powerful, and human.

08/05/26 How can we change the supply chain industry to become more innovative? To make the supply chain industry more innovative, technology shouldn't just be used to cut costs or speed up today's processes. It should redesign the end-to-end supply chain so data, decisions and execution are fully connected. Building a culture where frontline teams run small experiments, learn fast, and share what works — including lessons from responsible mistakes - and make sure you have the guardrails in place so AI-driven improvements can scale safely.

08/05/26 How can AI help rather than hinder empathy? AI can support empathy, not erode it — offering a non-judgemental space to share, while freeing people up to truly listen and be present. By taking on repetitive tasks, it gives time back for human connection. In healthcare, it eases documentation load, reduces burnout, and creates space for compassionate care. As AI handles the routine, soft skills like empathy and judgement become more valuable than ever. Brenda Vethanayagam, Partner, AI, Trust Leader, PwC Canada

08/05/26 How can AI streamline bureaucracy? AI cuts through bureaucracy by automating the routine, speeding up decisions, and connecting teams across silos. But the real power? Helping institutions rethink how decisions get made in the first place — surfacing insights, focusing teams on what matters, and coordinating action at scale. As AI takes on more of the workload, reskilling becomes essential — freeing people for roles where judgement and empathy are irreplaceable.
